title: Shoes - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Shoes 14 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Foster Children Women DAYTON COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a clothing closet with coats and shoes. Items available are dependent on donations and funding. Also accepts donations. 300 Flower Lane Dayton, OR 97114 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (503) 569-8788 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers clothing, linen, shoes, and grooming supplies. 450 SE Fairview Street Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Foster and adoptive children 0-18 years old Hours: 1st, 2nd, 3th and 5th Thursdays of the month 9am-11am; 4th Thursday 12:30pm-2:30pm Email (541) 848-0138 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HANDS AND FEET FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports foster families by providing trauma-informed TBRI training to help resource parents navigate the needs of children who have experienced neglect or abuse. To ease the transition into a new home, personalized care baskets filled with essentials, clothing, and toys, accompanied by a complimentary family dinner are provided. Eligibility: Resource parents and foster children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 519-4562 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, shoes, bedding, household items, and clothing for babies and newborns. Items are subject to availability. 1755 13th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday 9am-noon, closed holidays Email (503) 364-9936 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and clothing closet that includes shoes. Provides financial assistance for electric bills, prescriptions, and propane gas. Also provides bus tokens. Delivery is available for people who cannot come to the food pantry due to medical or transportation issues or other extenuating circumstances. 210 E 6th Avenue Junction City, OR 97448 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday 9am-1pm, Thursday 3pm-7pm Email (541) 998-3992 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROFESSIONAL WOMEN'S CLOTHES CLOSET CLOTHING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shoes and clothing for women seeking professional apparel for work. Clothing donations accepted. 14700 SE Rupert Drive Oak Grove, OR 97267 Eligibility: Women in need of clothing and shoes Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm Email (503) 654-3161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT 48 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tailored bags with essential items for foster children entering care, including clothing, hair care products, and LGBTQ2S+ sensitive options. Offers a six-week program for youth aging out of foster care, featuring workshops on financial literacy, resume building, and interview preparation. 2505 SE 11th Avenue Suite 122 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Foster youth ages 7-23 Hours: Varies Email (971) 275-7416 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM ANGELS LOVE BOX PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Fosters a supportive community for foster families. This includes caregivers, children in foster care, and any biological or adopted children living in the home. Volunteers or groups are matched with local foster families based on compatibility and location. Monthly visits provide support for the parents and a sense of normalcy, stability, and confidence for the children. 3340 Commercial Street SE Suite 110 Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Resource parents with at least one foster child in the home Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 877-6806 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST GOSPEL MISSION HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, shoes, clothing, and personal hygiene items. Blankets, rain ponchos, and umbrellas may also be available. 1999 N 7th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Dinner: Monday-Friday 5pm-6pm All other services: Monday-Friday noon-6pm Email (541) 269-5017 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SUNSET CHURCH HELPING HANDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing and shoes. 14986 NW Cornell Road Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday 9:30am-11:30am and 4pm-6pm. Closed the week of Christmas and New Year. Email (503) 292-9293 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FATHER'S HEART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in day center with services that include two meals a day with a sack lunch to-go, showers by appointment (not wheelchair accessible), haircuts, computer and telephone access, blankets, clothing, shoes, and hygiene items. Referrals to resources surrounding housing, mental health, and substance abuse. Assistance with the determination of homelessness letters to obtain an Oregon ID. 603 12th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Homeless adults age 18 and older and adults with families Hours: Day Center and meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 722-9780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing and shoes including interview-ready clothes, food, home goods, kitchenware (pots, pans, dish sets), and hygiene items. Workforce closet available with work boots and shoes, rain and safety gear. Shopping allowed once per month. Exceptions made for special circumstances. Accepts donations of food, clothing, bedding, linens, kitchenware, toys and games, and hygiene supplies. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Thrift Store: Monday/Wednesday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-noon and 1pm-3pm, 1st Saturday of the month noon-3pm -Donations: Visit website for current hours Email (541) 753-1420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A smaller version of Vina's Free Thrift Store with clothes, hygiene items, household goods, shoes, and more. Items vary by donation and are not guaranteed. Services are offered at two locations in collaboration with Alsea School District and South Benton Resource Center. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Must reside in area of closet locations and meet their eligibility criteria Hours: Monroe: Tuesday 10am-noon, South Benton Resource Center, 648 Orchard Street, Monroe, OR 97456 Alsea: By appointment with Alsea School District staff. Location will be provided when appointment is confirmed. Email (541) 487-5664 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides new and gently used clothing, shoes, toys, books, and supplies. Delivery is available to those living within 30 minutes of the Tigard facility. Scheduled pick-ups at the Tigard facility are available for others, Monday-Friday. There are textured hair care classes for transracial resource parents. 14555 SW 74th Avenue Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Resource parents with children in foster care ages 0-6 (up until 7th birthday) and after the child's case has changed to permanency Hours: By appointment Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details DAYTON COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY CLOTHING CLOSET FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID PROFESSIONAL WOMEN'S CLOTHES CLOSET CLOTHING SERVICES PROJECT 48 PROJECT 48 SALEM ANGELS LOVE BOX PROGRAM SOUTH COAST GOSPEL MISSION HOMELESS SERVICES SUNSET CHURCH HELPING HANDS THE FATHER'S HEART THE FATHER'S HEART VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT
